preview_text:
The model favors Canada strongly, assigning a 80% win probability against a 2% chance for Qatar. The underdog is given minimal probability of victory, though a draw registers at 18%, indicating some residual uncertainty about competitive balance.

The forecast implies a heavily skewed match shape. Canada's dominant win probability reflects an expected performance gap, yet the draw probability suggests this is not modeled as a blowout—Qatar has a genuine path to a result without defeat, even if outright victory appears remote by the model's assessment.

The model and sportsbooks diverge materially here. The books assign Qatar 15% win probability compared to the model's 2%—a gap of 13 percentage points—and credit a draw at 23% versus the model's 18%. Sportsbooks price Canada considerably tighter at 62% to the model's 80%. This substantial disagreement likely reflects different information or methodology; the dashboard offers no judgment on which source is more reliable.
